items.title: investingLive European FX news wrap: Eurozone PMIs stabilise, UK data disappoints
items.summary: Intervention risks weigh on momentum as USD/JPY approaches the highest level since 1986 Bitcoin turns more bearish with chip stocks nose diving ECB policymaker Lane sees signals pointing to price pressures in the coming months UK June flash services PMI 48.7 vs 50.1 expected Eurozone business activity sees a slower downturn in June as price pressures cool slightly Iran says it has no plans to let IAEA inspectors to visit nuclear sites Germany June flash manufacturing PMI 50.0 vs 50.4 expected French business downturn eases slightly in June, price pressures remain elevated French business climate see minimal rebound in June as struggles continue What are the main events for today? US futures hold lower as tech shares lead the drop again FX option expiries for 23 June 10am New York cut Core prices in Japan continue to keep above 2% in May, according to BOJ data The main highlight of the session were the Flash Eurozone and UK PMIs. The Eurozone June flash PMI painted a mixed picture, with services improving slightly to 48.9 versus 48.6 expected, suggesting the slowdown in activity may be stabilizing. In contrast, the UK services sector disappointed sharply, with June flash services PMI falling to 48.7 versus expectations of 50.1. The miss points to a notable loss of momentum in the UK economy, raising concerns that higher borrowing costs and weaker consumer demand are increasingly weighing on business activity, strengthening the case for a more cautious stance from the Bank of England. The Bank of Japan’s Core CPI gauge stayed above the 2% threshold in May. Although headline inflation remains partly distorted by government subsidies, the stickiness in underlying prices reinforces expectations that the BoJ will maintain its gradual tightening bias, especially as imported energy costs and a weaker yen continue to pose upside risks to inflation. Overall, the hawkish Fed shadow since last week is still driving the price action as nothing significant happened since then. In the American session, the only highlight will be the US Flash PMIs. Services PMI is expected to tick higher to 51.1 vs 50.7 prior, while the Manufacturing PMI is seen easing to 54.6 vs 55.1. This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 随着美元兑日元逼近1986年以来的最高水平，干预风险抑制了涨势；随着芯片股暴跌，比特币走势转为更趋看跌；欧洲央行决策者莱恩认为有迹象表明未来数月将面临价格压力；英国6月服务业PMI初值48.7，低于预期值50. 1）欧元区6月商业活动下滑速度放缓，因价格压力略有缓解 伊朗表示无计划允许国际原子能机构（IAEA） inspectors 访问核设施 德国6月制造业PMI初值50.0，低于预期值50.4 法国6月商业下滑幅度略有收窄，但价格压力仍居高不下 法国6月商业景气指数仅微幅反弹，经营困境持续 今日有哪些主要事件？受科技股再次领跌影响，美股期货维持低位；6月23日纽约时间上午10点外汇期权到期；据日本央行数据显示，5月日本核心通胀率继续维持在2%以上；本交易日的主要看点是欧元区和英国的PMI初值。欧元区6月初值PMI呈现喜忧参半的局面，服务业指数小幅回升至48.9（预期为48.6），表明经济活动放缓可能正在趋于稳定。 相比之下，英国服务业表现令人大失所望，6月服务业PMI初值降至48.7，低于预期值50.1。这一数据未达预期表明英国经济动能显著减弱，引发市场担忧：借贷成本上升和消费需求疲软正日益拖累商业活动，这进一步强化了英国央行采取更为审慎立场的理由。日本央行5月份的核心CPI指标仍维持在2%的门槛之上。尽管整体通胀水平在一定程度上仍受政府补贴的扭曲，但基础价格的粘性强化了市场对日本央行将维持渐进紧缩倾向的预期，尤其是进口能源成本上升和日元走弱继续给通胀带来上行风险。总体而言，自上周以来美联储的鹰派阴影仍在主导市场走势，因为此后并未发生任何重大事件。在美股交易时段，唯一的亮点将是美国初值PMI数据。服务业PMI预计将小幅升至51.1（前值50.7），而制造业PMI则预计将小幅回落至54.6（前值55.1）。本文作者为investinglive.com的朱塞佩·德拉莫塔（Giuseppe Dellamotta）。

Services PMI is expected to tick higher to 51.1 vs 50.7 prior, while the Manufacturing PMI is seen easing to 54.6 vs 55.1. This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 23 Jun: Stocks tumble as USD extends gain. Oil lower.
items.summary: Gold retests the 38.2% retracement and swing area McDonald's is now worth 160x rival Wendy's Crude oil futures settled below the 200 day moving average US treasury so $69 billion of two-year notes at a high yield of 4.189% U.S. Secretary of State Rubio: We're going to deal directly with the Lebanese government BOC's Macklem: So far we are not seeing much spreading of higher oil prices ECB's Vujcic: We do not want to give forward guidance, every meeting is live US Richmond Fed composite index +4 vs +13 prior US June S&P Global flash services PMI 51.3 vs 51.0 Bank of Canada's Macklem: We need to create more places for savings to go beyond the USA BOE's Taylor: An extended hold at this level is appropriate policy Memory stocks have had one of the all-time great runs. Why this could be the end Has the hype around SpaceX faded? Trump: I agreed to allow the Hormuz Strait to remain OPEN, with no further Naval Blockade investingLive European FX news wrap: Eurozone PMIs stabilise, UK data disappoints Intervention risks weigh on momentum as USD/JPY approaches the highest level since 1986 The early focus was on the sharp decline in U.S. equity futures, particularly the NASDAQ, which was down as much as 800 points in premarket trading. The selling pressure eased somewhat after the 9:30 a.m. ET open, but the damage was still significant. The NASDAQ fell to an intraday low of -653.34 points and closed down -579.56 points, or -2.21%. The index never traded in positive territory, with its best level of the day still lower by 284 points. The S&P 500 and Russell 2000 also ended the session in the red, falling -1.44% and -0.96%, respectively, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average was little changed, slipping just -0.09%. The decline in equities helped fuel a flight-to-safety bid for the U.S. dollar. The Dollar Index rose 0.37% on the day, with the biggest gains coming against the commodity currencies. The AUDUSD fell 1.23%, while the NZDUSD declined 0.86%, making them the largest movers among the major currency pairs. The USDJPY was the least affected, with the pair rising just 0.02% as traders remained wary of potential intervention from Japanese authorities. Yesterday, the pair nearly tested the 2024 high at 161.95, reaching 161.918, a level that represents the highest price since 1986. Although the pair is little changed today, it remains comfortably above its rising 100-hour moving average at 161.39 and its rising 200-hour moving average at 160.759. It would take a move below both of those levels to shift the short-term bias back to the downside. Until then, the buyers remain firmly in control. The USD was higher vs all the major currencies: EUR +0.42% GBP +0.37% CHF +0.11% CAD +0.38% AUD +1.23% NZD +0.82% On the economic front, the U.S. data was mixed. The S&P Global PMI report came in stronger than expected, with the services index rising to 51.3 versus 51.0 expected, manufacturing climbing to 55.7 versus 54.8 expected, and the composite index improving to 52.2 from 51.7 previously. The data suggested that economic activity continues to expand, but the details were less robust, with overall growth remaining modest, employment declining for a second consecutive month, and price pressures showing further signs of cooling. In contrast, the Richmond Fed survey painted a softer picture of the economy. The composite index fell to +4 from +13 previously, the services index dropped to -1 from +14, manufacturing shipments slowed to +3 from +16, and employment slipped to -1 from +3. While new orders and shipments remained in positive territory, the report marked a notable loss of momentum following recent strength. Meanwhile, Federal Reserve officials have remained notably quiet following last week's rate decision. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh has expressed a preference for less frequent public commentary from policymakers, and the lack of Fed chatter since the meeting suggests that approach is already influencing communications. Outside the U.S., Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem spoke as the USDCAD continued its powerful uptrend. The pair has rallied from an early May low of 1.3549 to a new yearly high of 1.42165, a move of nearly 668 pips, or 4.9%, in less than two months. Macklem did not address monetary policy directly, but instead highlighted growing global financial imbalances, noting that the United States continues to attract the largest share of global capital flows. He added that the enduring appeal of the U.S. dollar may have allowed these imbalances to persist longer than they otherwise would have, a reminder of the strong underlying demand supporting the greenback. To build a more balanced and resilient global financial system, policymakers need to create additional destinations for global savings beyond the U.S. At the same time, even as the U.S. has stepped back from free and open trade, other countries should seek to deepen their trade and investment relationships. He also addressed concern that increased leverage among hedge funds and non-financial firms may be making the financial system more fragile and susceptible to shocks. The remarks did not include any new signals on monetary policy or the economic outlook, but they underscored the continued strength of the U.S. dollar, with the USDCAD trading at the highest level since April 2025. The comments did not lead to any change in that trend with the price ending the day within 6 pips of the high for the day. In the US debt market, the yield curve steepened with the 2 year down -2.7 basis points to 4.202%, the 10 year down -0.6 basis points at 4.500%, but the 30 year up 0.4 basis points. Crude oil settled below the 200 day MA at $73.68 for the first time since the end of January. The price is trading at $73.23. Gold fell -$80 to $4109. Support is at $4000 to $4006. Silver fell -$3.51 or -$5.39% to $61.53. The low took out the low from June 11 and trades at the lowest level since March. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 黄金重新测试38.2%回调位及震荡区间 麦当劳当前市值是竞争对手温迪的160倍 原油期货收盘跌破200日移动平均线 美国财政部以4.189%的高收益率发行了690亿美元的两年期国债 美国国务卿鲁比奥： 我们将直接与黎巴嫩政府进行交涉 加拿大央行行长麦克莱姆：到目前为止，我们尚未看到油价上涨带来的显著传导效应 欧洲央行委员武伊奇奇： 我们不愿提供前瞻性指引，每次会议都是实时决策 美国里士满联储综合指数+4，前值+13 美国6月标普全球服务业PMI初值51.3，前值51.0 加拿大央行行长麦克莱姆：我们需要为储蓄创造更多流向美国以外市场的渠道 英国央行泰勒：在此水平维持利率不变是适当的政策 科技股曾经历过史上最辉煌的上涨行情之一。为何这可能是终点？围绕SpaceX的炒作是否已消退？特朗普：我同意保持霍尔木兹海峡畅通，不再实施海上封锁。investingLive欧洲外汇新闻综述：欧元区PMI趋稳，英国数据令人失望。随着美元兑日元逼近1986年以来的最高水平，干预风险抑制了涨势。早盘焦点集中于美国数据的急剧下滑。 股指期货，尤其是纳斯达克指数，在盘前交易中一度下跌800点。美国东部时间上午9:30开盘后，抛售压力有所缓解，但跌幅依然显著。纳斯达克指数盘中一度跌至-653.34点的低点，最终收跌-579.56点，跌幅达-2.21%。该指数全天未曾转为上涨，当日最高点仍较收盘价低284点。标普500指数和罗素2000指数也收跌，分别下跌1.44%和0.96%，而道琼斯工业平均指数基本持平，仅微跌0.09%。股市下跌助推了投资者对美元的避险需求。美元指数当日上涨0.37%，其中对大宗商品货币的涨幅最为显著。澳元兑美元下跌1.23%，新西兰元兑美元下跌0.86%，成为主要货币对中波动最大的两个。美元兑日元受影响最小，该货币对仅上涨0.02%，因交易员仍对日本当局可能采取的干预措施保持警惕。昨日，该货币对一度逼近2024年高点161.95，最高触及161.918，这一水平创下自1986年以来的最高价。 尽管该货币对今日波动不大，但仍稳稳位于161.39的上升中100小时移动平均线和160.759的上升中200小时移动平均线之上。只有当汇价跌破这两个水平时，短期走势才会重新转为看跌。在此之前，多头仍牢牢掌控着市场。美元兑所有主要货币均走高：欧元+0.42%、英镑+0.37%、瑞士法郎+0.11%、加元+0.38%、澳元+1.23%、新西兰元+0.82%。在经济方面，美国数据喜忧参半。标普全球PMI报告表现强于预期：服务业指数从预期的51.0升至51.3，制造业指数从预期的54.8升至55.7，综合指数从之前的51.7升至52.2。这些数据表明经济活动仍在扩张，但细节表现稍显疲软：整体增长依然温和，就业连续第二个月下滑，且价格压力进一步显现出降温迹象。相比之下，里士满联储的调查则描绘了一幅经济形势更为疲软的图景。 综合指数从之前的+13降至+4，服务业指数从+14降至-1，制造业出货量从+16放缓至+3，就业指数从+3滑落至-1。尽管新订单和出货量仍保持在正值区间，但该报告显示，在近期表现强劲之后，经济动能出现了明显减弱。与此同时，在上周利率决议公布后，美联储官员们一直保持着异常低调的态度。美联储新任主席凯文·沃什曾表示，他更倾向于政策制定者减少公开评论，而自会议以来美联储方面鲜有表态，这表明这种做法已开始影响其沟通方式。在美国以外，随着美元兑加元（USDCAD）延续强劲上涨趋势，加拿大央行行长蒂夫·麦克莱姆发表了讲话。该货币对从5月初的低点1.3549反弹至年内新高1.42165，在不到两个月的时间内上涨了近668点，涨幅达4.9%。麦克莱姆并未直接谈及货币政策，而是着重强调了日益加剧的全球金融失衡，并指出美国继续吸引着全球资本流动中的最大份额。他补充道，美国持久的吸引力 美元可能导致这些失衡状况持续的时间比原本更长，这提醒人们，支撑美元的潜在需求依然强劲。为了构建一个更加平衡且具有韧性的全球金融体系，政策制定者需要在美国之外为全球储蓄创造更多投资去向。与此同时，即使美国已从自由开放的贸易中退缩，其他国家也应寻求深化其贸易和投资关系。他还回应了外界的担忧，即对冲基金和非金融企业杠杆率的上升可能使金融体系更加脆弱，更容易受到冲击。这些言论并未就货币政策或经济前景发出任何新信号，但强调了美元的持续强势，美元兑加元汇率已升至2025年4月以来的最高水平。这些言论并未改变这一趋势，该汇率当日收盘价与日内高点仅相差6点。在美国债券市场，收益率曲线趋陡：2年期收益率下跌2.7个基点至4.202%，10年期收益率下跌0.6个基点至4.500%，但30年期收益率上涨0.4个基点。 原油收盘价跌至73.68美元，自1月底以来首次跌破200日均线。当前价格报73.23美元。黄金下跌80美元，至4109美元。支撑位在4000至4006美元之间。白银下跌3.51美元（跌幅5.39%），至61.53美元。盘中低点跌破6月11日的低点，目前处于3月以来的最低水平。本文作者为investinglive.com的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: BoJ June summary flag hikes ahead toward 1.75%
items.summary: Westpac warns of Middle East second-round CPI effects, still forecast an August RBA hike BOJ June summary flags faster hike calls as markets price another move by year-end. Recap. Australia May CPI undershoots on headline but core inflation tops forecasts at 3.6% Samsung $65bn buyback and new chip cluster talks spark South Korea equity bounce BofA lifts Nikkei target to 76,000 on AI demand, Hormuz relief and rising corporate ROE Australian May 2026 Headline CPI 4% y/y (vs. expected 4.3%), core 3.6% y/y (expected 3.5%) PBOC sets USD/ CNY mid-point today at 6.8195 (vs. estimate at 6.7) Anthropic's Mythos model exposes major flaws in classified US systems UBS sees market pricing of two Fed hikes as too aggressive ... get gold! Japan May services PPI holds at 3.3% as freight and air costs surge on fuel shock BOJ June summary reveals broad support for rate hike alongside sharp deflation warning TD joins big four banks flagging Australian inflation relief, all roads lead to RBA hold Gold faces $3,800 risk if Fed pivots to hikes, Deutsche Bank warns Retail investors chase AI upside as pros stay cautious and Nasdaq longs pile up UN shipping agency begins evacuating hundreds of ships stranded in Gulf via Hormuz Alphabet will replace Verizon in the Dow Jones Industrial Average Congress delivers historic but toothless war powers rebuke over Iran conflict Morgan Stanley North Haven fund pays out less than half of Q2 investor withdrawal requests investingLive Americas FX news wrap 23 Jun: Stocks tumble as USD extends gain. Oil lower. Private survey inventory shows a headline crude oil draw smaller than expected Session wrap summary points: Australia's May headline CPI slowed to 4.0%, well below the 4.3% forecast, but trimmed mean core inflation accelerated to 3.6% year-on-year, above estimates, keeping RBA August hike odds at around 36% and December at 67% The Australian dollar languished near 11-week lows and the kiwi sat at a seven-month trough as the mixed CPI data and broad USD strength weighed on antipodean currencies Gold extended losses as markets continued to price two to three Federal Reserve rate hikes over the next 12 months, with Fed Chair Warsh's hawkish signals from last week's FOMC meeting remaining the dominant narrative The BOJ's June Summary of Opinions showed some members calling for hikes every few months to reach neutral rate, with roughly 90% of economists now expecting another move by December and the cycle peak revised up to around 1.75% Samsung jumped as much as 10% in early Seoul trade on a Yonhap report of a 90 trillion won share buyback plan, with South Korea's government simultaneously flagging imminent announcements on a new AI chip cluster with Samsung and SK Hynix; gains faded as the morning progressed Morgan Stanley's $7 billion North Haven private credit fund said it would pay out less than half of Q2 redemption requests, capping exits at 5% as withdrawal demand hit 11.6% of shares Alphabet will replace Verizon in the Dow Jones Industrial Average ahead of Monday's open next week, S&P Global confirmed A split Australian inflation print set the tone for Asia-Pacific markets on Wednesday, with headline CPI undershooting forecasts but core measures accelerating, leaving the RBA's policy path unresolved and the Australian dollar pinned near 11-week lows. The kiwi fared no better, sitting at a seven-month trough as broad US dollar strength compounded the pressure on antipodean currencies. Westpac retained its August hike call, arguing that Middle East second-round effects are broadening across consumer prices in ways that the headline miss obscures, while markets settled on 36% odds for August and 67% for December. In Japan, the BOJ's June Summary of Opinions confirmed a board majority pushing toward neutral, with some members explicitly calling for hikes at intervals of a few months. Roughly 90% of economists now expect another move by December, and the projected cycle peak has been revised up to around 1.75%. The yen remains near 40-year lows despite the June hike, keeping import cost pressures alive and the hawks well-armed. Seoul provided the session's sharpest price action. Samsung surged as much as 10% early in the day on a Yonhap report of a 90 trillion won buyback plan, amplified by government confirmation of imminent announcements on a new AI semiconductor cluster with Samsung and SK Hynix. The gains faded as the morning wore on. Gold continued to slide, with the Fed hike narrative keeping the pressure on, and Morgan Stanley's North Haven private credit fund added to the sector's mounting liquidity headlines by capping Q2 redemptions at 5% against withdrawal demand of 11.6%. Away from macro, Alphabet will join the Dow Jones Industrial Average ahead of Monday's open, replacing Verizon. Oil inventory data ahead of official data due Wednesday morning in the US showed the SPR at its lowest level in more than 40 years. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 西太平洋银行警告中东局势将引发第二轮CPI影响，仍预测澳大利亚储备银行将于8月加息；日本央行6月摘要显示加息呼声高涨，市场已将年底前再次加息纳入定价。回顾：澳大利亚5月CPI总指数低于预期，但核心通胀率达3.6%，高于预期；三星650亿美元股票回购计划及新建芯片产业集群谈判提振韩国股市反弹；美银受AI需求推动将日经指数目标价上调至76,000点， 霍尔木兹海峡局势缓和以及企业净资产收益率（ROE）上升。澳大利亚5月2026年通胀数据：整体CPI同比4%（预期4.3%），核心CPI同比3.6%（预期3.5%）。中国人民银行今日将美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8195（市场预期为6.7） Anthropic公司的Mythos模型揭露了美国机密系统中的重大缺陷 瑞银认为市场对美联储两次加息的预期过于激进…… 抢购黄金！日本5月服务业生产者物价指数（PPI）维持在3.3%，因燃料冲击导致运费和航空成本飙升 日本央行6月摘要显示，市场普遍支持加息，同时发出严峻通缩警告 多伦多道明银行（TD）加入四大银行行列，指出澳大利亚通胀压力有所缓解，种种迹象均指向澳大利亚央行将维持利率不变 若美联储转向加息，黄金将面临跌破3,800美元的风险， 德意志银行警告：专业投资者保持谨慎，纳斯达克多头头寸持续累积，散户却追逐AI概念；联合国海运机构开始通过霍尔木兹海峡疏散数百艘滞留海湾的船舶；Alphabet将取代Verizon进入道琼斯工业平均指数；美国国会对伊朗冲突发出具有历史意义但缺乏实效的战争权力谴责；摩根士丹利北黑文基金支付的赎回款不足投资者第二季度赎回请求总额的一半；investingLive美洲外汇新闻综述6月23日：美元延续涨势，股市暴跌，油价走低。 私人调查数据显示，原油总库存降幅小于预期 交易日总结要点： 澳大利亚5月整体CPI增速放缓至4.0%，远低于4.3%的预期，但剔除波动项的核心通胀率同比加速至3.6%，高于市场预期，这使得市场对澳大利亚央行8月加息的概率维持在36%左右，12月加息概率为67%。澳元徘徊在11周低点附近，新西兰元则处于七 月低点，因喜忧参半的CPI数据及美元全面走强对南半球货币构成压力。黄金延续跌势，因市场继续定价未来12个月美联储将加息两至三次，上周FOMC会议上美联储主席沃什发出的鹰派信号仍是市场的主导叙事。日本央行6月《意见摘要》显示，部分委员呼吁每隔数月加息一次以达到中性利率， 目前约90%的经济学家预计12月前将再次加息，加息周期峰值被上调至约1.75%。据韩联社报道，三星计划实施90万亿韩元的股票回购计划，受此消息提振，三星股价在首尔早盘交易中一度大涨10%；与此同时，韩国政府也暗示将很快宣布与三星及SK海力士合作建设新的AI芯片集群；但随后涨幅收窄，因 随着上午交易推进，摩根士丹利旗下规模达70亿美元的北黑文（North Haven）私募信贷基金表示，将仅满足第二季度赎回申请的不到一半，并将赎回额度上限设定为5%，因赎回需求已达到基金份额的11.6%。Alphabet将于下周一开盘前取代Verizon进入道琼斯工业平均指数， 标普全球确认。周三，澳大利亚通胀数据喜忧参半，为亚太市场定下基调：整体CPI低于预期，但核心通胀加速，这使得澳大利亚储备银行（RBA）的政策路径仍不明朗，澳元汇率被压制在11周低点附近。新西兰元表现同样不佳，徘徊在七个月低点，因美元全面走强加剧了对南半球货币的压力。西太平洋银行维持了8月加息的预期，认为中东问题的二次影响正在消费价格中蔓延，而这一情况被整体CPI数据低于预期所掩盖；与此同时，市场对8月加息的概率预期定为36%，对12月加息的概率预期定为67%。在日本，日本央行6月的《意见摘要》证实，理事会多数成员倾向于转向中性立场，部分成员甚至明确呼吁以数月为间隔加息。 目前约90%的经济学家预计12月前将再次加息，且本轮加息周期的峰值预测已上调至约1.75%。尽管6月已加息，日元汇率仍徘徊在40年低点附近，这使得进口成本压力持续存在，也让鹰派人士占据上风。首尔股市当天的价格波动最为剧烈。受韩联社报道三星将推出90万亿韩元股票回购计划的提振，加之韩国政府证实即将宣布与三星、SK海力士合作建设新的人工智能半导体集群，三星股价盘初一度大涨10%。但随着上午交易时段的推进，涨幅逐渐收窄。黄金价格持续下跌，美联储加息预期持续施压；摩根士丹利旗下北黑文（North Haven）私募信贷基金将第二季度赎回额度上限设定为5%，而赎回需求高达11.6%，这进一步加剧了该行业日益严峻的流动性问题。在宏观层面之外，Alphabet将于周一开盘前加入道琼斯工业平均指数，取代威瑞森（Verizon）。在美国周三上午公布官方数据之前，原油库存数据显示，战略石油储备（SPR）已降至40多年来的最低水平。本文作者为investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan。
items.summaryEn: Westpac warns of Middle East second-round CPI effects, still forecast an August RBA hike BOJ June summary flags faster hike calls as markets price another move by year-end. Recap. Australia May CPI undershoots on headline but core inflation tops forecasts at 3.6% Samsung $65bn buyback and new chip cluster talks spark South Korea equity bounce BofA lifts Nikkei target to 76,000 on AI demand, Hormuz relief and rising corporate ROE Australian May 2026 Headline CPI 4% y/y (vs. expected 4.3%), core 3.6% y/y (expected 3.5%) PBOC sets USD/ CNY mid-point today at 6.8195 (vs. estimate at 6.7) Anthropic's Mythos model exposes major flaws in classified US systems UBS sees market pricing of two Fed hikes as too aggressive ... get gold! Japan May services PPI holds at 3.3% as freight and air costs surge on fuel shock BOJ June summary reveals broad support for rate hike alongside sharp deflation warning TD joins big four banks flagging Australian inflation relief, all roads lead to RBA hold Gold faces $3,800 risk if Fed pivots to hikes, Deutsche Bank warns Retail investors chase AI upside as pros stay cautious and Nasdaq longs pile up UN shipping agency begins evacuating hundreds of ships stranded in Gulf via Hormuz Alphabet will replace Verizon in the Dow Jones Industrial Average Congress delivers historic but toothless war powers rebuke over Iran conflict Morgan Stanley North Haven fund pays out less than half of Q2 investor withdrawal requests investingLive Americas FX news wrap 23 Jun: Stocks tumble as USD extends gain. The kiwi fared no better, sitting at a seven-month trough as broad US dollar strength compounded the pressure on antipodean currencies. Westpac retained its August hike call, arguing that Middle East second-round effects are broadening across consumer prices in ways that the headline miss obscures, while markets settled on 36% odds for August and 67% for December. In Japan, the BOJ's June Summary of Opinions confirmed a board majority pushing toward neutral, with some members explicitly calling for hikes at intervals of a few months. Roughly 90% of economists now expect another move by December, and the projected cycle peak has been revised up to around 1.75%. The yen remains near 40-year lows despite the June hike, keeping import cost pressures alive and the hawks well-armed. Seoul provided the session's sharpest price action. Samsung surged as much as 10% early in the day on a Yonhap report of a 90 trillion won buyback plan, amplified by government confirmation of imminent announcements on a new AI semiconductor cluster with Samsung and SK Hynix. The gains faded as the morning wore on. Gold continued to slide, with the Fed hike narrative keeping the pressure on, and Morgan Stanley's North Haven private credit fund added to the sector's mounting liquidity headlines by capping Q2 redemptions at 5% against withdrawal demand of 11.6%. Away from macro, Alphabet will join the Dow Jones Industrial Average ahead of Monday's open, replacing Verizon. Oil inventory data ahead of official data due Wednesday morning in the US showed the SPR at its lowest level in more than 40 years. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.impactZh: 央行政策预期变化可能提升主要货币对波动，杠杆仓位的保证金与回撤压力上升。
items.impactEn: Shifts in central-bank expectations may increase major-pair volatility and raise margin/drawdown pressure on leveraged positions.
